title: lichen_l2_input accepts frames with src_eui64 from untrusted parser output without validation
description:
Location: lichen_l2.c:641-657

**What goes wrong:** The lichen_link_rx() call writes to src_eui64[8] (line 645), but this buffer is stack-allocated and the return value only indicates success/failure. If lichen_link_rx() has a bug where it returns success but doesn't fully populate src_eui64, the LOG_DBG at line 654 prints uninitialized stack data.

**Impact:** Minor - just log garbage. But if src_eui64 is used for routing decisions in future (comment says "Unknown sender" today), uninitialized values could cause misdirected packets.

**Fix:** Zero-initialize src_eui64 before passing to lichen_link_rx(), or add explicit validation that it was populated on success.
